data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description:
1. CZ: New book "Freedom of Money" will be released next week; ebook pre-orders are now open.
2. Bitcoin mining company Riot sold 3,778 BTC in Q1 2026.
3. Trump says the new Iranian regime must act quickly.
4. CZ: All proceeds from the sale of his new book "Freedom of Money" will be donated to charity.
5. Trader loracle.hl is selling 450,000 HYPE, worth $15.98 million.
6. Intergaze will officially shut down; users must complete asset withdrawals within 14 days before the bridge closes.
7. A whale sold 1,323 ETH after holding them for two months, incurring a loss of $1.28 million.

data.title: Polymarket's Revenue Ranks Fifth Among Crypto Protocols
data.description:
Polymarket has achieved a notable position in the crypto sector, ranking fifth in daily revenue among crypto protocols, with earnings reaching $1.71 million, according to BlockBeats. This development follows the introduction of a new fee structure on March 30, which imposes Taker fees across nearly all trading categories for the first time.

The new fee system features variable rates, with peak fees for cryptocurrency-related contracts reaching up to 1.8%. Actual fees fluctuate based on share prices and market dynamics. Categories such as sports, finance, politics, culture, weather, and general predictions have lower tiered rates, while certain economic predictions and mention-based categories have higher peak rates, approximately 1.5%.

In response to community inquiries about whether the platform will support POLY token payments for fee discounts, Polymarket team member Mustafa confirmed that this option will be available.

data.title: NYSE and Nasdaq Closed for Good Friday, Trading to Resume April 6
data.description: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) and Nasdaq were closed on April 3 in observance of Good Friday, marking one of Nasdaq's ten full-day closures in 2026. According to NS3.AI, while the stock exchanges remained closed, banks continued their operations. Stock trading is scheduled to resume on Monday, April 6, at 9:30 a.m. ET.

data.title: OKX will support upgrades to Polygon, Kaia, and Flare networks.
data.description: According to official news, OKX will support the upgrades of the Polygon, Kaia, and Flare networks. To ensure a smooth upgrade, the platform will suspend deposit and withdrawal services for the Kaia network from 08:30 (UTC+8) on April 7, 2026; for the Polygon network from 21:30 (UTC+8) on April 8, 2026; and for the Flare network from 19:30 (UTC+8) on April 14, 2026. Please do not deposit or withdraw any tokens during the network upgrade period to avoid financial losses. Deposit and withdrawal services will resume once the network upgrade is complete and running stably, without further announcement. Trading of the relevant tokens will not be affected during the upgrade, but please be aware of market volatility risks.

data.title: Spot CVD Chart Analysis for BTC/USDT Pair on April 3
data.description: The Spot CVD chart provides an analysis of the order book for the BTC/USDT spot pair as of 5:00 a.m. UTC on April 3. According to NS3.AI, this chart integrates a volume heatmap with a Cumulative Volume Delta (CVD) view, which distinguishes buy and sell orders based on trade size.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts USD.ai's Market Valuation
data.description: Polymarket has forecasted significant market valuations for USD.ai following its launch. According to Foresight News, the prediction market indicates a 92% probability that USD.ai's fully diluted valuation (FDV) will exceed $100 million the day after its launch. There is also an 85% chance of surpassing $150 million and a 50% likelihood of exceeding $200 million. The current trading volume in this prediction market has surpassed $2.5 million.

data.title: U.S. Government Job Openings Decline in February Amid Labor Market Concerns
data.description: U.S. government job openings decreased by 51,000 in February, reaching a total of 701,000, marking the second-lowest level since December 2020. According to NS3.AI, this decline has raised concerns about the labor market's health. The Conference Board's March survey revealed a drop in the labor market differential to 5.8 points. Analysts are closely monitoring the upcoming March jobs report for indications of further weakness in the employment sector.

data.title: Iran: Second US F-35 fighter jet shot down
data.description: According to Iran's Mehr News Agency, Iran claims that a second US F-35 fighter jet was shot down over central Iran, and that the pilot's chances of survival are slim. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Whales Face Significant Losses, Reports Glassnode
data.description: Bitcoin whales holding between 100 and 10,000 BTC are experiencing substantial losses, according to Foresight News. Glassnode tweeted that the seven-day moving average of realized losses has exceeded $200 million daily.

data.title: Iran Claims Second U.S. F-35 Fighter Jet Shot Down
data.description: Iran has announced the downing of a second U.S. F-35 fighter jet over central Iran. According to Odaily, the Iranian authorities have stated that the likelihood of the pilot surviving is minimal.

data.title: International Monetary Fund: Tokenization improves financial efficiency but introduces new risks, potentially weakening emerging market currency sovereignty.
data.description: A 23-page report released by the IMF on Thursday noted that tokenization has the potential to eliminate financial friction and improve transparency, but its net effect on financial stability remains uncertain. The report stated that while atomic settlement and increased transparency reduce some traditional risks, speed and automation introduce new risks. Stress events in tokenized markets may unfold faster than in traditional systems, leaving less time for human intervention. The IMF also stated that tokenization offers emerging markets opportunities to accelerate cross-border payments and financial inclusion, but it also brings risks of increased capital flow volatility, rapid currency substitution, and weakened monetary sovereignty. Furthermore, the report pointed out that without legal clarity regarding the finality of ownership records and settlements, the tokenized market may face fragmentation and marginalization. Currently, the total value of on-chain tokenized real-world assets (excluding stablecoins) exceeds $27.6 billion. Boston Consulting Group estimated in 2022 that the tokenized market could reach $16 trillion by 2030, while McKinsey gave a more conservative forecast of $2 trillion in 2024.

data.title: U.S. to Impose 100% Tariffs on Certain Imported Pharmaceuticals
data.description: The White House announced on Thursday that it will impose a 100% tariff on certain imported pharmaceuticals. According to Ming Pao, this move is aimed at pressuring pharmaceutical companies to expand their production within the United States. The decision includes several key exemptions to mitigate potential impacts on the healthcare sector. The administration's strategy seeks to bolster domestic manufacturing and reduce dependency on foreign drug supplies.

data.title: Grayscale Submits S-1 Amendment for Bittensor Trust
data.description: Grayscale has submitted an S-1 amendment for its Bittensor (TAO) Trust, according to SEC filings. According to BlockBeats, this development marks a significant step in Grayscale's efforts to expand its offerings in the cryptocurrency sector. The amendment aims to provide more detailed information about the trust, which is part of Grayscale's broader strategy to offer diverse investment products in the digital asset space.

data.title: A whale sold 1,323 ETH after holding them for two months, incurring a loss of $1.28 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale sold 1,323 ETH for 2.7 million USDC at a price of $2,041 after holding them for two months, incurring a loss of $1.28 million. This whale initially withdrew 1,327.48 ETH from Binance, worth $3.99 million, and currently still holds 5.76 ETH, worth $11,800.

data.title: CZ: All proceeds from the sale of my new book, *Freedom of Money*, will be donated to charity.
data.description: CZ posted on the X platform that all proceeds from the sale of his new book, *Freedom of Money*, will be donated to charity, and that the book was not published for profit. Previously, it was reported that *Freedom of Money* was scheduled for official release next week, and the ebook is currently available for pre-order in both English and Traditional Chinese.

data.title: U.S. March Employment Report Anticipated to Show Growth
data.description: The U.S. March non-farm employment report is set to be released tonight at 20:30, with market expectations indicating an increase of 60,000 jobs, marking a shift from previous negative growth trends. According to ChainCatcher, while U.S. stocks and international gold, silver, and oil markets remain closed today, the data's significance for the Federal Reserve's interest rate trajectory could potentially trigger market activity next Monday. Investors are advised to be mindful of associated risks.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $71.1688 million yesterday, with BlackRock's ETHA experiencing the largest net outflow at $46.6635 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $71.1688 million yesterday (April 2nd, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock ETF ETHA, with a net outflow of $46.6635 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow has reached $11.563 billion. This was followed by Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF ETHE, with a single-day net outflow of $16.8039 million. ETHE's historical total net outflow has reached $5.177 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $11.699 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Ethereum market capitalization) of 4.69%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.481 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $8.9926 million yesterday, with Fidelity FBTC leading the way with a net inflow of $7.2935 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $8.9926 million yesterday (April 2nd, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was Fidelity ETF (FBTC), with a net inflow of $7.2935 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $10.961 billion. This was followed by VanEck ETF (HODL), with a net inflow of $4.7368 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.177 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was BlackRock ETF (IBIT), with a net outflow of $3.0377 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $63.115 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $86.223 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.44%, and a cumulative net inflow of $55.957 billion.

data.title: Pension-usdt.eth Increases Short Position with Leverage
data.description: On April 3, pension-usdt.eth significantly increased its short position in Ethereum using three times leverage, according to BlockBeats On-chain Detection. The entity currently holds a total short position of approximately $107.5 million in Bitcoin and Ethereum.

data.title: Survey Reveals Gen Z's Concerns Over Crypto Scams on Social Media in Japan
data.description: A recent survey conducted by Clabo involving 1,486 participants in Japan highlights that Generation Z is more concerned about cryptocurrency scams on social media compared to older age groups. According to NS3.AI, the survey also revealed that 23.3% of all respondents perceive cryptocurrencies as suspicious due to a lack of understanding of how they function.
